title: Diocese of Pensacola-Tallahassee Cybersecurity Policy
summary: This policy establishes the guidelines, standards, roles, and procedures governing cybersecurity risk management, data protection, access control, incident response, and technology service provider oversight for the Diocese of Pensacola-Tallahassee.
notes: 1) The 'rev. August, 2022' header notation has been interpreted as the last review/revision date; no explicit effective date is stated, so effective_date is left null. 2) No explicit next-review date or review cadence for the policy itself is stated; individual compliance tasks range from quarterly to every three years (sections 6.1–6.3), but these refer to operational reviews, not policy review cycles. 3) Retention period of 3 years is inferred from the Log Analysis Report retention requirement in section 16.0 (15.4); raw logs are retained 12 months. These are log-specific, not a general policy retention period — a human reviewer should confirm the applicable diocesan records-retention schedule. 4) The document uses section numbering inconsistency: section 16.0 is titled 'Log Management' but its subsections are numbered 15.1–15.5, suggesting a drafting/renumbering error that should be corrected. 5) The CFO is designated as primary policy owner with broad authority, but the CTO/CISO holds significant operational responsibility; the diocese may wish to clarify a single accountable owner. 6) Section 12.0 states (as of October 2021) that the diocese does not accept, store, or review HIPAA-classified information — this assertion should be periodically revalidated given the diocese's potential exposure through HR benefit programs. 7) No formal version number is assigned in the document; only the revision date is noted. 8) Several substantive sub-policies are referenced but not embedded (Remote Access Policy, Acceptable Use Policy, Document Retention Policy, Incident Response Plan, Cybersecurity Risk Assessment Policy, Personal Communication Devices Policy) — a complete policy library audit is recommended to ensure all referenced documents exist and are current. 9) The suggested chapter/section address is a best guess pending knowledge of the diocese's actual policy numbering framework.
